reasoning: Candidate A is the stronger relative choice because it preserves the primary business path: a skeptical family planner can understand the service, see the no-login safety model, and start a free alert in the first viewport on both desktop and mobile. Candidate B has a more strategically distinctive coverage-confidence angle and better paid-search intent matching, but its mobile first viewport delays the form and CTA too far; screenshot 6 shows the form only beginning below the first fold, which weakens the immediate conversion path. On desktop, both have excessive top whitespace, but A’s first viewport still co-locates the trust proof and full form more effectively. B has lower trope risk because the restaurant coverage grid is a domain-specific structural move rather than a generic SaaS proof section, but that does not compensate for the weaker mobile conversion path.
best_reusable_move_a: Hero co-location of the no-login trust proof, official handoff, benign four-field alert form, and free CTA; this directly resolves the trust blocker at the action point.
best_reusable_move_b: Coverage grid as both search-intent confirmation and Pro upsell motivation; seeing multiple desired restaurants makes unlimited alerts feel naturally valuable.
